ideation: This animation sequence depicts the careful and precise movements of a person using a sewing needle.  The primary focus is on the right arm, which manipulates the needle, while the left arm provides support.  The sequence will highlight the delicate nature of the task, using subtle movements to convey precision and control.  The robot's lack of fingers will be addressed by simulating the action of holding and guiding the needle with the entire hand, rather than individual fingers. Frame 1 will show the robot's arms in a neutral position, ready to begin. Frame 2 will have the left hand supporting the fabric (using a slight inward rotation to imply holding). Frame 3 will show the right arm moving the needle downward, piercing the fabric (using a slight downward angle and inward rotation to imply the needle's action). Frame 4 will have the right arm moving the needle upward, bringing the needle out of the fabric. Frame 5 will show the arms returning to a neutral position, ready for the next stitch.

frames.beat: arms rest, ready for the first stitch
left hand steadies the fabric, right arm poised
right arm guides the needle down, piercing cloth
right arm pulls the needle up, completing the stitch
both arms return to rest, ready for the next
